obligations.description: Protect the personality and fundamental rights of natural persons whose personal data is processed.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: This Act applies to the processing of personal data of natural persons by private persons and federal bodies.
obligations.subject: private persons, federal bodies

obligations.description: This Act does not apply to personal data processed by a natural person exclusively for personal use.
obligations.subject: natural person

obligations.description: This Act does not apply to personal data processed by the Federal Assembly and parliamentary committees as part of their deliberations.
obligations.subject: Federal Assembly, parliamentary committees

obligations.description: This Act does not apply to personal data processed by institutional beneficiaries under Article 2 paragraph 1 of the Host State Act who enjoy immunity from jurisdiction in Switzerland.
obligations.subject: institutional beneficiaries under the Host State Act

obligations.description: Procedural law regulates the processing of personal data and data subject's rights in court proceedings and federal procedural proceedings, but this Act applies to first instance administrative proceedings.
obligations.subject: administrative authorities

obligations.description: Public registers for private legal transactions and access to them shall be regulated by specific federal laws, and this Act applies only if those laws do not contain rules.
obligations.subject: federal lawmakers, public register administrators

obligations.description: The Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC) is obligated to supervise the application of federal data protection regulations.
obligations.subject: Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC)

obligations.description: Personal data must be processed lawfully.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing must be carried out in good faith and be proportionate.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data may only be collected for a specific, recognizable purpose and further processed only in a compatible manner.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data must be destroyed or anonymised when no longer required for the processing purpose.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must ensure the accuracy of personal data and take appropriate measures to correct, delete, or destroy inaccurate or incomplete data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Consent must be given voluntarily, for one or more specific processing instances, and based on appropriate information.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Consent must be explicitly given for processing sensitive personal data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Consent must be explicitly given for high-risk profiling by a private person.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Consent must be explicitly given for profiling by a federal body.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must design data processing in technical and organizational terms to comply with data protection regulations, particularly the principles in Article 6, starting from the planning stage.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must implement technical and organizational measures appropriate to the state of the art, the nature and extent of processing, and the risks to the data subject's personality or fundamental rights.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must ensure default settings limit personal data processing to the minimum necessary for the intended purpose, unless the data subject specifies otherwise.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller and processor must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of data security commensurate with the risk.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The measures taken must be capable of preventing data security breaches.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The Federal Council is authorised to issue provisions establishing minimum requirements for data security.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: A processor may only process personal data in the manner permitted to the controller.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: A processor may only be assigned processing if no statutory or contractual confidentiality duty prohibits it.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must ensure the processor can guarantee data security.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A processor may only subcontract processing to a third party with the prior approval of the controller.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: A processor may rely on the same legal grounds for justification as the controller.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: Private controllers may appoint a data protection officer.
obligations.subject: private controller

obligations.description: The data protection officer must serve as the contact point for data subjects and Swiss data protection authorities.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: The data protection officer must train and advise the private controller on data protection matters.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: The data protection officer must provide support on applying data protection regulations.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: The data protection officer must exercise their function independently and without instructions from the controller.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: The data protection officer must not perform activities incompatible with their duties.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: The data protection officer must possess the required expertise.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: The private controller must publish the data protection officer's contact details and notify the FDPIC.
obligations.subject: private controller

obligations.description: The Federal Council shall regulate the appointment of data protection officers by federal bodies.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: Professional, industry, and trade associations authorized to safeguard economic interests may submit codes of conduct to the FDPIC.
obligations.subject: professional, industry and trade associations

obligations.description: The FDPIC must state and publish opinions on submitted codes of conduct.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The controller shall maintain a record of processing activities including specific details such as identity, purpose, categories of data subjects and data, recipients, retention period, security measures, and international data transfers.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The processor shall maintain a record of processing activities including the identity of the processor and controller, categories of processing carried out on behalf of the controller, and details on security measures and international data transfers.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: Federal bodies shall notify the FDPIC of their records of processing activities.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: The Federal Council may exempt legal entities with fewer than 250 employees from record-keeping obligations if their data processing poses a negligible risk to data subjects' personality.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: Manufacturers of data processing systems or programs, controllers, and processors may have their systems, products, and services evaluated by recognised independent certification bodies.
obligations.subject: manufacturers, controllers, processors

obligations.description: The Federal Council shall issue regulations on the recognition of certification procedures and the introduction of a data protection quality mark.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The Federal Council shall take account of international law and internationally recognised technical standards when issuing regulations on certification procedures and the data protection quality mark.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: Private controllers with a registered office or domicile abroad must appoint a representative in Switzerland if they process personal data of individuals in Switzerland and the processing meets specified criteria including connection to offering goods/services or monitoring behavior, large scale, re
obligations.subject: private controller

obligations.description: The appointed representative must serve as the contact point for data subjects and the Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC).
obligations.subject: representative

obligations.description: The controller shall publish the name and address of the appointed representative.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The representative shall maintain a record of the controller's processing activities containing the information specified in Article 12 paragraph 2.
obligations.subject: representative

obligations.description: The representative shall provide the FDPIC with the information contained in the record upon request.
obligations.subject: representative

obligations.description: The representative shall provide data subjects with information on how they can exercise their rights upon request.
obligations.subject: representative

obligations.description: Personal data may only be disclosed abroad if the Federal Council has determined that the foreign state or international body provides an adequate level of data protection.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: In the absence of an adequate level of protection determination by the Federal Council, personal data may only be disclosed abroad if one of the specified alternative safeguards is in place.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Data protection clauses in agreements between the controller/processor and their contractual partner must be notified to the FDPIC beforehand.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Specific guarantees drawn up by the competent federal body must be notified to the FDPIC beforehand.
obligations.subject: competent federal body

obligations.description: Standard data protection clauses must be approved, issued, or recognized by the FDPIC beforehand to be valid for cross-border data transfers.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: Binding corporate rules must be approved in advance by the FDPIC or by a data protection authority in a country with an adequate level of protection.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: The Federal Council may establish additional appropriate safeguards for cross-border data transfers, consistent with the criteria in paragraph 2.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if the data subject has explicitly consented to the disclosure.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if the disclosure is directly connected with the conclusion or performance of a contract between the controller and the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if the disclosure is directly connected with the conclusion or performance of a contract between the controller and its contractual partner in the interests of the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if necessary to safeguard an overriding public interest.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if necessary to establish, exercise, or enforce legal rights before a court or another competent foreign authority.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if necessary to protect the life or physical integrity of the data subject or a third party, and consent cannot be obtained within a reasonable time.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if the data subject has made the data generally accessible and has not explicitly prohibited processing.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Personal data may be disclosed abroad if the data originate from a statutory register that is public or accessible to persons with a legitimate interest, provided statutory access requirements are met.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: The controller or processor shall inform the FDPIC upon request about the disclosure of personal data under paragraph 1 letters b number 2, c, and d.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: The controller shall inform the data subject in an appropriate manner when collecting personal data, even if the data is not collected from the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall provide the data subject with minimum information including its identity and contact details, the purpose of processing, and if applicable, the recipients or categories of recipients of the data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When personal data is not collected from the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject of the categories of processed personal data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When personal data is disclosed abroad, the controller shall inform the data subject of the state or international body to which the data is disclosed and, if applicable, the guarantees under Article 16 paragraph 2 or the application of an exception under Article 17.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When personal data is not collected from the data subject, the controller shall provide the information specified in paragraphs 2–4 no later than one month after receiving the data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: If the controller discloses the personal data before the one-month deadline for informing the data subject, it shall inform the data subject prior to disclosure.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from the duty to provide information if the data subject already possesses the information.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from the duty to provide information if the processing is required by law.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from the duty to provide information if it is a private person legally required to preserve confidentiality.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from the duty to provide information if the conditions of Article 27 are met.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from the duty to provide information when personal data is not collected from the data subject and providing it is impossible.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from the duty to provide information when personal data is not collected from the data subject and providing it would require disproportionate effort.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may restrict, delay, or dispense with providing information if overriding third-party interests require it.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may restrict, delay, or dispense with providing information if disclosure would defeat the purpose of the processing.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A private controller may restrict, delay, or dispense with providing information if it has overriding interests and does not intend to disclose the data to third parties.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A federal body may restrict, delay, or dispense with providing information to protect Switzerland's internal or external security.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject about any automated individual decision that has a legal consequence or considerable adverse effect on them.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must allow the data subject, upon request, to express their point of view regarding an automated individual decision.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to request that an automated individual decision be reviewed by a natural person.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The obligation to provide information and allow expression of viewpoint does not apply if the automated decision is necessary for contract performance and the data subject's request is granted.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The obligation to provide information and allow expression of viewpoint does not apply if the data subject has explicitly consented to automated decision-making.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: If issued by a federal body, the automated individual decision must be designated as such.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: The requirement to allow the data subject to express their point of view does not apply if federal administrative procedure law denies the right to a hearing before the decision.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall carry out a data protection impact assessment before processing that is likely to result in a high risk to the data subject's personality or fundamental rights.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A joint data protection impact assessment may be carried out when several similar processing procedures are planned.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data protection impact assessment must include a description of the planned processing, an evaluation of risks to the data subject's personality or fundamental rights, and a description of protective measures.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Private controllers are exempt from the obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment if they are legally required to process the data.
obligations.subject: private controller

obligations.description: A private controller may dispense with a data protection impact assessment if using a certified system, product, or service under Article 13 or complying with a qualifying code of conduct under Article 11.
obligations.subject: private controller

obligations.description: A code of conduct under Article 11 must be based on a data protection impact assessment to qualify for exemption from the assessment requirement.
obligations.subject: code of conduct developer

obligations.description: The controller must seek the FDPIC's opinion before proceeding with processing that poses a high risk to personality or fundamental rights, despite planned mitigation measures.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The FDPIC must inform the controller of any objections to the planned processing within two months, with a possible one-month extension for complex processing.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: If the FDPIC objects to the planned processing, it must propose suitable measures to the controller.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: A private controller may skip consulting the FDPIC if it has already consulted its data protection officer under Article 10.
obligations.subject: private controller

obligations.description: The controller shall notify the FDPIC of any data security breach likely to lead to a high risk to the data subject's personality or fundamental rights as quickly as possible.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall, in the notification to the FDPIC, specify at minimum the nature of the breach, its consequences, and the measures taken or planned.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The processor shall notify the controller of any data security breach as quickly as possible.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: The controller shall inform the data subject if such notification is required for their protection or if the FDPIC so requests.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may limit, delay, or dispense with informing the data subject if certain conditions under Article 26, impossibility, disproportionate effort, or public announcement apply.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: With the controller’s consent, the FDPIC may forward the breach notification to the National Cyber Security Centre for analysis, even if it contains sensitive personal data.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: Any person may request information from the controller on whether their personal data is being processed.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject is entitled to receive specific information including the controller's identity, processed data, purpose of processing, retention period, data source, automated decision-making logic, and recipients of data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject may consent to having their health data communicated by a health professional of their choice.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller remains obligated to provide information even when personal data is processed by a processor.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: No one may waive their right to information in advance.
obligations.subject: any person

obligations.description: The controller must provide information free of charge, with possible exceptions defined by the Federal Council.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may refuse to provide, restrict, or delay information if a formal law permits it, particularly to preserve professional secrecy.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may refuse to provide, restrict, or delay information if necessary to safeguard overriding third-party interests.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may refuse to provide, restrict, or delay information if the request is obviously unjustified, such as when it does not serve data protection purposes or is frivolous.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A private person controller may refuse, restrict, or delay information if their own overriding interests require it and they do not intend to disclose the data to third parties.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A federal body controller may refuse, restrict, or delay information if required to protect overriding public interests, such as Switzerland's internal or external security.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A federal body controller may refuse, restrict, or delay information if disclosure could compromise an enquiry, investigation, or administrative or judicial proceedings.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Legal entities within the same corporate group are not considered third parties for the purpose of restricting information under paragraph 2(a)(2).
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide a reason for refusing, restricting, or delaying the provision of information.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A controller may refuse, restrict, or delay providing information about personal data processed exclusively for editorial publication if it reveals information sources.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A controller may refuse, restrict, or delay providing information about personal data processed exclusively for editorial publication if it would allow access to publication drafts.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A controller may refuse, restrict, or delay providing information about personal data processed exclusively for editorial publication if it would compromise public freedom to shape opinions.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Journalists may refuse, restrict, or delay providing information about personal data used exclusively as an aid to their personal work.
obligations.subject: journalists

obligations.description: A data subject may request the controller to deliver their personal data in a conventional electronic format if the processing is automated and based on consent or contract performance.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: A data subject may request the controller to transfer their personal data to another controller if the conditions in paragraph 1 are met and no disproportionate effort is required.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller must deliver or transfer personal data free of charge, unless the Federal Council establishes exceptions for disproportionate effort.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The Federal Council may establish exceptions to the free delivery or transfer requirement if the effort is disproportionate.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The controller may refuse, restrict, or delay the delivery or transfer of personal data based on the grounds listed in Article 26 paragraphs 1 and 2.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide reasons for refusing, restricting, or delaying the delivery or transfer of personal data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Any person who processes personal data must not unlawfully breach the data subjects' personality rights.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data must not be processed contrary to the principles of Articles 6 and 8.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data must not be processed contrary to the express wishes of the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Sensitive personal data must not be disclosed to third parties.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: No breach of personality rights arises if the data subject makes personal data generally accessible and has not explicitly prohibited any processing.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must not process sensitive personal data or engage in high-risk profiling under this provision.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may disclose data to third parties only if those parties need the data to conclude or perform a contract with the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must ensure that processed data are no more than ten years old.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject must have attained the age of majority for this provision to apply.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must process personal data professionally and exclusively for publication in an editorial section or as an aid to personal work.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When processing data for research, planning, or statistics, the controller must anonymize the data as soon as possible, or implement appropriate measures to prevent identification if anonymization is impossible or disproportionately burdensome.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: If sensitive personal data are involved in research, planning, or statistics, the controller must disclose them in a way that prevents identification of the data subject, or ensure third parties use them only for non-personal purposes.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must publish results of research, planning, or statistics in a manner that does not identify data subjects.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject may request correction of incorrect personal data, unless prohibited by statute or for public interest archiving.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject may request prohibition of a specific data processing activity.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject may request prohibition of specific disclosure of personal data to third parties.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject may request deletion or destruction of personal data.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: If the accuracy of personal data cannot be established, the data subject may request that the data be marked as disputed.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject may request that corrections, deletions, prohibitions, markings as disputed, or judgments be communicated to or published for third parties.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The Federal Council shall regulate control procedures and responsibility for data protection when federal bodies jointly process personal data with other federal bodies, cantonal bodies, or private persons.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: Federal bodies may only process personal data if there is a statutory basis for doing so.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: A statutory basis in a formal law is required when processing sensitive personal data.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: A statutory basis in a formal law is required when processing involves profiling.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: A statutory basis in a formal law is required when the purpose or manner of processing may lead to a serious violation of the data subject's fundamental rights.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: A statutory basis in a substantive law is sufficient for processing sensitive personal data or profiling if processing is essential for a task required by a formal law and poses no particular risks to fundamental rights.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies may process personal data without a statutory basis if the Federal Council has authorized the processing due to no risk to data subject rights.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies may process personal data if the data subject has consented to the processing in the specific case or has made their data generally accessible without explicitly prohibiting processing.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies may process personal data if it is necessary to protect life or physical integrity and consent cannot be obtained within a reasonable time.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: The Federal Council may authorize automated processing of sensitive personal data during a pilot trial only if the processing is required by an existing formal law, adequate measures minimize rights violations, and a test phase is essential for practical implementation.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The Federal Council must obtain the opinion of the FDPIC before authorizing a pilot trial involving automated data processing.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The competent federal body must submit an evaluation report to the Federal Council within two years of the start of the pilot trial, proposing whether to continue or discontinue the processing.
obligations.subject: competent federal body

obligations.description: Automated data processing must be discontinued if no formal enactment establishing a legal basis comes into force within five years of the pilot trial's start.
obligations.subject: competent federal body

obligations.description: Federal bodies may only disclose personal data if there is a statutory basis under Article 34 paragraphs 1–3.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies may disclose personal data without a statutory basis under Article 34 if one of the specified derogation conditions (a–e) is met.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies must ensure the data subject is given an opportunity to comment before disclosure under condition 2e, unless impossible or disproportionate.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies may disclose personal data as part of official information or under the Freedom of Information Act if the data relates to public duties and there is an overriding public interest.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: A data subject may object to the disclosure of specific personal data by a federal body if they credibly demonstrate a legitimate interest.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: A federal body must reject an objection to data disclosure if there is a legal duty to disclose the data.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: A federal body must reject an objection to data disclosure if fulfilling its tasks would otherwise be jeopardised.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: Federal bodies shall offer to the Federal Archives all personal data that they no longer regularly require.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies shall destroy personal data that the Federal Archives do not deem to be worth archiving, unless the data are anonymised or must be preserved for evidentiary, security, or legitimate interest purposes.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies must anonymize personal data as soon as the purpose of processing permits when processing for non-specific purposes such as research, planning, or statistics.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: Federal bodies may only disclose sensitive personal data to private persons in a manner that ensures data subjects are not identifiable.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: Recipients of sensitive personal data may only transmit the data to third parties with the consent of the federal body that disclosed the data.
obligations.subject: recipient

obligations.description: Results of data processing must be published in a manner that ensures data subjects are not identifiable.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: Articles 6(3), 34(2), and 36(1) of the Data Protection Act do not apply to data processing under this article.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: When a federal body acts under private law, it must comply with the provisions on data processing applicable to private persons.
obligations.subject: federal body

obligations.description: Any person with a legitimate interest may request the responsible federal body to stop the unlawful processing of personal data.
obligations.subject: responsible federal body

obligations.description: Any person with a legitimate interest may request the responsible federal body to redress the consequences of unlawful processing of personal data.
obligations.subject: responsible federal body

obligations.description: Any person with a legitimate interest may request the responsible federal body to declare the processing of personal data to be unlawful.
obligations.subject: responsible federal body

obligations.description: The responsible federal body shall correct, delete, or destroy personal data upon request by an applicant with a legitimate interest.
obligations.subject: responsible federal body

obligations.description: The responsible federal body shall communicate its decisions regarding correction, deletion, or destruction of data, objections to disclosure, or marking data as disputed to third parties or publish them.
obligations.subject: responsible federal body

obligations.description: The responsible federal body shall restrict processing instead of deleting or destroying personal data if the data subject disputes accuracy and it cannot be established, or if overriding interests of third parties, public security, or ongoing procedures require it.
obligations.subject: responsible federal body

obligations.description: The responsible federal body shall mark personal data as disputed if neither its accuracy nor inaccuracy can be established.
obligations.subject: responsible federal body

obligations.description: The data subject may assert rights under Article 41 in proceedings concerning access to official documents containing personal data.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The United Federal Assembly shall elect the head of the FDPIC (the Commissioner).
obligations.subject: United Federal Assembly

obligations.description: Any person with the right to vote on federal matters is eligible for election as Commissioner.
obligations.subject: Eligible persons

obligations.description: The Commissioner's employment relationship shall be governed by the Federal Personnel Act unless otherwise provided by this Act.
obligations.subject: Employment authorities

obligations.description: The Commissioner shall be insured against financial consequences of retirement, invalidity, and death with PUBLICA.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: If the Commissioner remains in office after age 65 and requests it, pension coverage shall be extended until the end of the employment contract, but no later than age 68.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall finance the employer’s contributions for the Commissioner’s pension insurance.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The Federal Assembly shall issue implementing provisions on the Commissioner’s employment contract via ordinance.
obligations.subject: Federal Assembly

obligations.description: The Commissioner shall exercise duties independently without seeking or accepting instructions from any authority or third party.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The Commissioner shall be assigned for administrative purposes to the Federal Chancellery.
obligations.subject: Federal Chancellery

obligations.description: The Commissioner shall have a permanent secretariat and his or her own budget.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The Commissioner shall appoint his or her staff.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The Commissioner's term of office is four years and may be extended twice, beginning on the first day of January following the start of the National Council's legislature period.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The Commissioner may terminate their employment contract at the end of any month with six months' notice, unless a shorter notice is granted by the Judiciary Committee under exceptional circumstances.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The United Federal Assembly may remove the Commissioner from office before the end of the term if they have wilfully or through gross negligence committed a serious violation of official duties or have permanently lost the capacity to perform them.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The Judiciary Committee may issue a reprimand to the Commissioner if it determines they have failed to comply with official duties.
obligations.subject: Judiciary Committee

obligations.description: The FDPIC must submit the annual draft budget via the Federal Chancellery to the Federal Council.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The Federal Council must submit the budget received from the FDPIC unchanged to the Federal Assembly.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The Commissioner may not be a member of the Federal Assembly or the Federal Council.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The Commissioner may not have any other employment relationship with the Confederation.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The Commissioner may not hold any additional occupations unless permitted by the Judicial Committee under specific conditions.
obligations.subject: Commissioner

obligations.description: The Judicial Committee may permit the Commissioner to carry out an additional occupation only if it does not adversely affect the exercise of duties, independence, or reputation of the FDPIC.
obligations.subject: Judicial Committee

obligations.description: Decisions by the Judicial Committee permitting additional occupations must be published.
obligations.subject: Judicial Committee

obligations.description: In case of a dispute regarding the Commissioner’s recusal, the president of the competent division of the Federal Administrative Court shall decide.
obligations.subject: President of the division of the Federal Administrative Court

obligations.description: The FDPIC must implement suitable control measures, particularly regarding data security, to ensure legally compliant implementation of federal data protection regulations within their office.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall open an investigation into a federal body or private person if there are sufficient indications of a potential violation of data protection regulations.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC may refrain from opening an investigation if the violation of data protection regulations is of minor importance.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The federal body or private person shall provide the FDPIC with all information and documents needed for the investigation.
obligations.subject: federal body or private person

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall inform the data subject who filed a report about the steps taken and the result of any investigation.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC may order access to all information, documents, records of processing activities, and personal data required for an investigation if the federal body or private person fails to cooperate.
obligations.subject: federal body or private person

obligations.description: The FDPIC may order access to premises and installations as part of an investigation if the federal body or private person fails to cooperate.
obligations.subject: federal body or private person

obligations.description: The FDPIC may question witnesses as part of an investigation if the federal body or private person fails to cooperate.
obligations.subject: federal body or private person

obligations.description: The FDPIC may obtain appraisals by experts as part of an investigation if the federal body or private person fails to cooperate.
obligations.subject: federal body or private person

obligations.description: Professional secrecy must be respected during investigations by the FDPIC.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC may request support from other federal authorities and cantonal or communal police to enforce investigation measures.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: Investigation proceedings and rulings under Articles 50 and 51 must follow the Administrative Procedure Act (APA).
obligations.subject: competent authority

obligations.description: Only the federal body or private person against whom an investigation has been opened may be a party to the proceedings.
obligations.subject: competent authority

obligations.description: The FDPIC may contest appeal decisions issued by the Federal Administrative Court.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: Federal administrative authorities must invite the FDPIC to comment before issuing rulings on data protection issues.
obligations.subject: Federal administrative authorities

obligations.description: If the FDPIC and another authority are investigating the same party, they must coordinate their proceedings.
obligations.subject: FDPIC and the other federal administrative authority

obligations.description: Federal and cantonal authorities must provide the FDPIC with information and personal data necessary for it to fulfill its statutory duties.
obligations.subject: federal authorities and cantonal authorities

obligations.description: The FDPIC must provide information and personal data to Swiss data protection authorities when required for their statutory duties.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC must provide information and personal data to competent prosecution authorities when the matter relates to an offence reported under Article 65 paragraph 2.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC must provide information and personal data to federal, cantonal, and communal police for implementing measures under Articles 50 paragraph 3 and 51.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC may exchange information or personal data with foreign data protection authorities only if reciprocity in administrative assistance is guaranteed.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC may exchange information or personal data with foreign data protection authorities only if the data are used solely for the data protection proceedings specified in the request.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The recipient foreign authority must undertake to preserve professional, trade, and manufacturing secrecy.
obligations.subject: recipient authority

obligations.description: The recipient foreign authority may only disclose the received information or personal data to third parties with the prior approval of the providing authority.
obligations.subject: recipient authority

obligations.description: The recipient foreign authority must comply with all requirements and restrictions imposed by the authority that provided the information or personal data.
obligations.subject: recipient authority

obligations.description: The FDPIC may provide the identity of data subjects only if the data subjects have consented or if disclosure is essential for the FDPIC or the foreign authority to fulfill its statutory duties.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall maintain a register of processing activities carried out by federal bodies.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall publish the register of processing activities of federal bodies.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall submit an annual report on its activities to the Federal Assembly and the Federal Council.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall publish the annual report on its activities.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall inform the public about its findings and rulings in cases of general interest.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall inform, train, and advise federal bodies and private persons on data protection matters.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall support cantonal bodies and collaborate with Swiss and foreign data protection authorities.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall raise public awareness, particularly among vulnerable persons, regarding data protection.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall provide data subjects, upon request, with information on how to exercise their rights.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall comment on draft federal legislation and measures involving data processing.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall carry out duties assigned under the Freedom of Information Act or other federal acts.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall develop working instruments and recommendations of good practice for controllers, processors, and data subjects, considering field specifics and protection of vulnerable persons.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC may advise federal bodies not subject to its supervision under Articles 2 and 4.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: Federal bodies may allow the FDPIC to inspect their files.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall charge fees to private persons for providing opinions on codes of conduct.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall charge fees to private persons for approving standard data protection clauses and binding corporate rules.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall charge fees to private persons for consultation related to data protection impact assessments.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall charge fees to private persons for implementing precautionary measures and measures under Article 51.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC shall charge fees to private persons for providing advice on data protection issues.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The Federal Council shall specify the amount of the fees charged by the FDPIC.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The Federal Council may stipulate cases in which fees may be waived or reduced.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: Private persons must not wilfully provide false or incomplete information in violation of their duties under Articles 19, 21, and 25–27.
obligations.subject: private persons

obligations.description: Private persons must wilfully provide accurate and complete information to the data subject as required by Articles 19(1) and 21(1).
obligations.subject: private persons

obligations.description: Private persons must wilfully provide the data subject with the specific information outlined in Article 19(2).
obligations.subject: private persons

obligations.description: Private persons must not wilfully provide false information to the FDPIC or wilfully fail to cooperate during an investigation, as required by Article 49(3).
obligations.subject: private persons

obligations.description: Private persons must not disclose personal data abroad in violation of Article 16(1) and (2) without meeting the requirements of Article 17.
obligations.subject: private persons

obligations.description: Private persons must not assign data processing to a processor without satisfying the requirements of Article 9(1) and (2).
obligations.subject: private persons

obligations.description: Private persons must comply with the minimum requirements for data security stipulated by the Federal Council in Article 8(3).
obligations.subject: private persons

obligations.description: Any person who wilfully discloses secret personal data acquired during the practice of their profession shall be liable to a fine not exceeding 250,000 francs.
obligations.subject: professional

obligations.description: Any person who wilfully discloses secret personal data acquired while carrying on an activity for or while training with a person subject to a duty of confidentiality shall be liable to a fine not exceeding 250,000 francs.
obligations.subject: person carrying on an activity for or training with a duty-bound entity

obligations.description: The disclosure of secret personal data after ceasing to practise a profession or after completing training is prohibited and constitutes a criminal offence.
obligations.subject: former professional or trainee

obligations.description: Any private person who wilfully fails to comply with a ruling issued by the FDPIC or a decision of the appeal courts referencing this Article shall be liable to a fine not exceeding 250,000 francs.
obligations.subject: private person

obligations.description: Businesses may be held criminally liable for administrative offenses under Articles 6 and 7 of the Federal Act on Administrative Criminal Law.
obligations.subject: business

obligations.description: If a fine of no more than 50,000 francs is involved and identifying the individual perpetrator would be disproportionate, the authority may impose the fine directly on the business instead of pursuing individuals.
obligations.subject: authority

obligations.description: The FDPIC may file a complaint with the competent prosecution authority for criminal acts related to data protection.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: The FDPIC may exercise the rights of a private claimant in criminal proceedings related to data protection.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: Prosecution for violations under the FADP is subject to a five-year statute of limitations.
obligations.subject: prosecuting authorities

obligations.description: The Federal Council may conclude international treaties to facilitate cooperation between data protection authorities.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The Federal Council may conclude international treaties to mutually acknowledge adequate levels of data protection for cross-border data disclosure.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: Processing of personal data that began before the Act came into force is exempt from Articles 7, 22, and 23 if the purpose remains unchanged and no new data are collected.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Ongoing investigations by the FDPIC at the time the Act comes into force are governed by the previous law.
obligations.subject: FDPIC

obligations.description: Appeals pending against first-instance decisions issued before the Act comes into force are governed by the previous law.
obligations.subject: courts or appellate authorities

obligations.description: Federal bodies may continue to process and disclose data of legal entities under existing federal legislation for five years after the Act comes into force.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: Federal bodies must have a legal basis authorizing the disclosure of personal data to continue disclosing data of legal entities under transitional provisions.
obligations.subject: federal bodies

obligations.description: The election and termination of the Commissioner's office shall be governed by the previous law until the end of the legislature period in which this Act comes into force.
obligations.subject: relevant authorities responsible for electing and terminating the Commissioner

obligations.description: If the incumbent is elected in the first vote of the United Federal Assembly, the new term of office begins on the day after the election.
obligations.subject: United Federal Assembly

obligations.description: If the Commissioner’s employment contract was based on the previous law, the previous law continues to apply to that contract.
obligations.subject: relevant contractual parties (e.g., employer and Commissioner)

obligations.description: The Act is subject to an optional referendum.
obligations.subject: state

obligations.description: The Federal Council shall determine the commencement date of the Act.
obligations.subject: Federal Council

obligations.description: The Federal Act of 19 June 1992 on Data Protection is repealed.
obligations.subject: state

obligations.description: The Schengen-Data Protection Act of 28 September 2018 is repealed.
obligations.subject: state

obligations.description: This Regulation lays down rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and rules relating to the free movement of personal data.
obligations.subject: Regulation (EU)

obligations.description: This Regulation protects fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons, in particular their right to the protection of personal data.
obligations.subject: Regulation (EU)

obligations.description: The free movement of personal data within the Union shall be neither restricted nor prohibited for reasons connected with the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data.
obligations.subject: Member States or other entities

obligations.description: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data by automated means or non-automated means if part of a filing system.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: This Regulation does not apply to processing of personal data in activities outside the scope of Union law.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: This Regulation does not apply to processing by Member States when carrying out activities under Chapter 2 of Title V of the TEU.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: This Regulation does not apply to processing by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity.
obligations.subject: natural person

obligations.description: This Regulation does not apply to processing by competent authorities for criminal prevention, investigation, detection, prosecution, or public security purposes.
obligations.subject: competent authorities

obligations.description: Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 applies to processing of personal data by Union institutions, bodies, offices, and agencies.
obligations.subject: Union institutions, bodies, offices, and agencies

obligations.description: Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 and other Union legal acts applicable to processing by Union institutions must be adapted to the principles and rules of this Regulation.
obligations.subject: Union institutions, bodies, offices, and agencies

obligations.description: This Regulation does not override the application of Directive 2000/31/EC, particularly its liability rules for intermediary service providers.
obligations.subject: intermediary service providers

obligations.description: The Regulation applies to the processing of personal data in the context of activities of an establishment of a controller or processor in the Union, regardless of where the processing occurs.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The Regulation applies to processing of personal data of data subjects in the Union by a controller or processor not established in the Union, if the processing relates to offering goods or services to such data subjects.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The Regulation applies to processing of personal data of data subjects in the Union by a controller or processor not established in the Union, if the processing relates to monitoring their behaviour within the Union.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The Regulation applies to processing of personal data by a controller not established in the Union but in a place where Member State law applies under public international law.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Controllers or processors transferring personal data to third countries or international organisations must make binding and enforceable commitments via contractual or other legally binding instruments to apply appropriate safeguards, including data subject rights.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: Certification under this Article must be voluntary and available through a transparent process.
obligations.subject: certification body

obligations.description: Certification under this Article does not reduce the responsibility of the controller or processor for compliance.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly, and in a transparent manner with respect to the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data must be collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner incompatible with those purposes.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Further processing for archiving, scientific or historical research, or statistical purposes is permitted if compliant with Article 89(1).
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data must be adequate, relevant, and limited to what is necessary for the purposes for which they are processed.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data must be accurate and kept up to date, with reasonable steps taken to erase or rectify inaccurate data without delay.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data must be stored in a form that permits identification of data subjects for no longer than necessary for the processing purposes.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data may be stored longer for archiving, scientific or historical research, or statistical purposes if appropriate technical and organisational measures are implemented.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of personal data is lawful only if at least one of the six specified legal bases applies.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must ensure that processing is based on the data subject's consent for specific purposes.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing is permitted when necessary to perform a contract with the data subject or to take steps at their request prior to contract formation.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must ensure processing is necessary to comply with a legal obligation to which they are subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing is permitted when necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or another natural person.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing is permitted when necessary for the performance of a task in the public interest or exercise of official authority vested in the controller.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing is permitted for legitimate interests pursued by the controller or a third party, unless overridden by the data subject's fundamental rights and freedoms, especially if the data subject is a child.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Public authorities are prohibited from relying on legitimate interests (Article 6(1)(f)) as a legal basis for processing carried out in the performance of their tasks.
obligations.subject: public authority

obligations.description: Member States may introduce more specific provisions to adapt the application of the legal bases under Article 6(1)(c) and (e).
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: The controller must assess the compatibility of further processing with the original purpose by considering factors such as the link between purposes, context of collection, nature of data, consequences for data subjects, and existence of safeguards.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Union or Member State law may restrict the scope of data subject rights and controller obligations under Articles 12–22 and Article 34, provided the restriction respects fundamental rights and is necessary and proportionate for safeguarding national security, defence, or public security.
obligations.subject: Union or Member State legislature

obligations.description: The controller must be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented to the processing of their personal data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Consent must be presented in a clearly distinguishable, intelligible, and easily accessible form using clear and plain language when included in a written declaration covering other matters.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Any part of a consent declaration that violates the GDPR is not binding.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to withdraw their consent at any time.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject must be informed before giving consent that they have the right to withdraw it.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The process for withdrawing consent must be as easy as the process for giving consent.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When assessing whether consent is freely given, account must be taken of whether contract performance is conditional on consent to processing not necessary for that performance.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of a child's personal data for information society services is lawful only if the child is at least 16 years old, or if consent is given or authorized by the holder of parental responsibility when the child is under 16.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Member States may set a lower age threshold for lawful processing of children's data, but not below 13 years.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: The controller must make reasonable efforts to verify that consent for processing a child's data is given or authorized by the holder of parental responsibility, considering available technology.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The provisions of Article 8 do not override Member States' general contract law regarding the validity, formation, or effect of contracts involving children.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of special categories of personal data, including racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data for unique identification, health data, and data concerning sex life or sexual orientation, is prohibited.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of special categories of personal data is permitted if the data subject has given explicit consent for one or more specified purposes, unless prohibited by Union or Member State law.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of special categories of personal data is permitted when necessary for carrying out obligations and exercising specific rights under employment, social security, or social protection law, as authorized by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement with appropriate safeguards.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of special categories of personal data is permitted when necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or another natural person, where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of special categories of personal data is permitted when carried out in the course of legitimate activities by a not-for-profit body with appropriate safeguards.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences must be carried out only under the control of official authority or when authorised by Union or Member State law with appropriate safeguards for data subjects' rights and freedoms.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Any comprehensive register of criminal convictions must be kept only under the control of official authority.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A controller is not obliged to maintain, acquire, or process additional information solely to identify a data subject when processing does not require identification.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: If a controller cannot identify a data subject, it must inform the data subject accordingly, where possible.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Data subjects may exercise their rights under Articles 15 to 20 only if they provide additional information enabling their identification.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Articles 15 to 20 do not apply to a controller who cannot identify the data subject, unless the data subject provides identifying information.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide information to the data subject in a concise, transparent, intelligible, and easily accessible form using clear and plain language, especially when addressed to a child.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide information in writing or by other means, including electronic means, and may provide it orally if the data subject's identity is verified.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must facilitate the exercise of data subject rights under Articles 15 to 22.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall not refuse to act on a data subject's request to exercise rights under Articles 15 to 22 unless it can demonstrate it cannot identify the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must respond to requests under Articles 15 to 22 without undue delay and within one month of receipt.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may extend the response period by two further months if necessary due to complexity or number of requests, and must inform the data subject of the extension and reasons within one month of receiving the request.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with the identity and contact details of the controller and, where applicable, the controller’s representative at the time personal data are obtained.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with the contact details of the data protection officer, where applicable, at the time personal data are obtained.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of the purposes of the processing and the legal basis for the processing at the time personal data are obtained.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Where processing is based on legitimate interests under Article 6(1)(f), the controller must inform the data subject of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or a third party at the time personal data are obtained.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of the recipients or categories of recipients of the personal data, if any, at the time personal data are obtained.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Where the controller intends to transfer personal data to a third country or international organisation, the controller must inform the data subject of the existence or absence of an adequacy decision, or reference to appropriate safeguards and how to obtain them, at the time personal data are obtai
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with the period for which the personal data will be stored, in addition to the information in paragraph 1, at the time personal data are obtained.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with the identity and contact details of the controller and, where applicable, the controller’s representative.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with the contact details of the data protection officer, where applicable.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of the purposes of the processing and the legal basis for it.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of the categories of personal data being processed.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of the recipients or categories of recipients of the personal data, if any.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject if it intends to transfer personal data to a third country or international organization, including the existence or absence of an adequacy decision, and reference to appropriate safeguards and how to obtain them.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with the period for which the personal data will be stored, or if not possible, the criteria used to determine that period.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to obtain confirmation from the controller as to whether their personal data are being processed.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to access their personal data when they are being processed.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with information on the purposes of the processing of their personal data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with information on the categories of personal data being processed.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must provide the data subject with information on the recipients or categories of recipients to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed, especially in third countries or international organisations.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must, where possible, provide the data subject with the envisaged period for which the personal data will be stored, or if not possible, the criteria used to determine that period.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of their right to request rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, or objection to processing.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of their right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When personal data are not collected from the data subject, the controller must provide any available information about their source.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject of the existence of automated decision-making, including profiling, and provide meaningful information about the logic, significance, and envisaged consequences of such processing.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to obtain without undue delay the rectification of inaccurate personal data concerning them.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to have incomplete personal data completed, including by providing a supplementary statement.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller must rectify inaccurate or incomplete personal data without undue delay.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to obtain erasure of their personal data without undue delay under specified conditions.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller shall erase personal data without undue delay when one of the specified legal grounds applies.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When personal data have been made public, the controller shall take reasonable steps, including technical measures, to inform other controllers processing the data that the data subject has requested erasure.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to request restriction of processing when personal data accuracy is contested, for a period allowing the controller to verify accuracy.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to request restriction of processing when processing is unlawful and they oppose erasure but wish to restrict use instead.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to request restriction of processing when the controller no longer needs the data but the data subject requires it for legal claims.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to request restriction of processing when they object to processing under Article 21(1), pending verification of legitimate grounds.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller must restrict processing of personal data as requested by the data subject under the conditions specified in paragraph 1.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Personal data under restriction may only be processed with the data subject’s consent or for legal claims, protection of rights, or important public interest, excluding storage.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject before lifting a restriction on processing.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must communicate any rectification, erasure, or restriction of personal data to each recipient to whom the data were disclosed, unless it is impossible or involves disproportionate effort.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must inform the data subject about the recipients to whom the data were disclosed if the data subject requests it.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to receive their personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to transmit their personal data to another controller without hindrance from the original controller.
obligations.subject: data subject
