obligations.description: The data subject has the right to have personal data transmitted directly from one controller to another where technically feasible.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller must provide personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format upon request by the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must not hinder the data subject's transmission of personal data to another controller.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The right to data portability does not apply to processing necessary for public interest or official authority tasks.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The exercise of the right to data portability must not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to object at any time to processing of personal data based on Article 6(1)(e) or (f), including profiling, on grounds relating to their particular situation.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller must cease processing personal data unless it demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds that override the data subject's interests, rights, and freedoms, or for legal claims.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject has the right to object at any time to processing of personal data for direct marketing purposes, including profiling related to such marketing.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The controller must cease processing personal data for direct marketing purposes upon objection by the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must explicitly bring the data subject's right to object (under paragraphs 1 and 2) to their attention at the latest during the first communication, presented clearly and separately from other information.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The data subject may exercise the right to object through automated means using technical specifications when using information society services.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data subject has the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, that produces legal effects or similarly significantly affects them.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: A decision based solely on automated processing is permitted if it is necessary for entering into or performing a contract with the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A decision based solely on automated processing is permitted if authorized by Union or Member State law that includes suitable safeguards for the data subject’s rights and interests.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A decision based solely on automated processing is permitted if based on the data subject’s explicit consent.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: When automated decisions are based on contract performance or explicit consent, the controller must implement suitable measures to safeguard the data subject’s rights, including the right to human intervention, to express their point of view, and to contest the decision.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Automated decisions shall not be based on special categories of personal data under Article 9(1), unless Article 9(2)(a) or (g) applies and suitable safeguards are in place.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure and demonstrate compliance with the GDPR, based on the nature, scope, context, and purposes of processing, as well as the associated risks.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall review and update the implemented technical and organisational measures where necessary.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Where proportionate to the processing activities, the controller shall implement appropriate data protection policies.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller may use adherence to approved codes of conduct or certification mechanisms as an element to demonstrate compliance with its obligations.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to implement data-protection principles such as data minimisation and integrate safeguards to meet GDPR requirements and protect data subjects' rights.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall ensure that, by default, only the personal data necessary for each specific processing purpose are collected, processed, stored, and made accessible.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller shall ensure that personal data are not made accessible to an indefinite number of natural persons without the individual's intervention by default.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: An approved certification mechanism under Article 42 may be used to demonstrate compliance with the requirements of paragraphs 1 and 2.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Joint controllers must transparently determine their respective responsibilities for compliance with GDPR obligations, particularly regarding data subject rights and information provision under Articles 13 and 14, via an arrangement.
obligations.subject: joint controllers

obligations.description: Joint controllers must ensure that the arrangement reflecting their roles and relationships vis-à-vis data subjects is duly documented and its essence made available to data subjects.
obligations.subject: joint controllers

obligations.description: Joint controllers may designate a contact point for data subjects in their arrangement.
obligations.subject: joint controllers

obligations.description: Data subjects may exercise their rights under GDPR against each joint controller individually, regardless of the terms of the arrangement between them.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Controllers or processors not established in the Union must designate a written representative in the Union when Article 3(2) applies.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Controllers or processors are exempt from designating a representative if their processing is occasional, not large-scale, does not involve special categories of data or criminal conviction data, and is unlikely to risk data subjects' rights and freedoms.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Public authorities or bodies are exempt from the obligation to designate a representative under Article 27(1).
obligations.subject: public authority or body

obligations.description: The representative must be established in a Member State where data subjects whose data are processed reside.
obligations.subject: representative

obligations.description: The representative must be mandated to act on behalf of the controller or processor in communications with supervisory authorities and data subjects regarding compliance with the GDPR.
obligations.subject: representative

obligations.description: Designating a representative does not prevent legal actions from being taken against the controller or processor themselves.
obligations.subject: supervisory authorities or data subjects

obligations.description: The controller shall use only processors that provide sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures ensuring compliance with the GDPR and protection of data subjects' rights.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The processor shall not engage another processor without prior specific or general written authorisation from the controller.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: When general authorisation is given, the processor shall inform the controller of any intended changes regarding the addition or replacement of other processors, allowing the controller to object.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: Processing by a processor shall be governed by a binding contract or legal act under Union or Member State law that specifies the subject-matter, duration, nature, purpose, types of data, categories of data subjects, and the obligations and rights of the controller.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The processor shall process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, including regarding transfers to third countries or international organisations, unless required by Union or Member State law.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: The contract or other legal act between controller and processor may be based on standard contractual clauses issued by the Commission or a supervisory authority.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The Commission may establish standard contractual clauses for data processing matters under the examination procedure in Article 93(2).
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: A supervisory authority may adopt standard contractual clauses for data processing matters under the consistency mechanism in Article 63.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The contract or other legal act between controller and processor must be in writing, including in electronic form.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: If a processor determines the purposes and means of processing, it shall be considered a controller and held liable accordingly.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: Processors and persons acting under the authority of the controller or processor shall not process personal data except on instructions from the controller, unless required by Union or Member State law.
obligations.subject: processor, person acting under the authority of the controller or processor

obligations.description: Processors and persons acting under the authority of the controller or processor are permitted to process personal data if required by Union or Member State law.
obligations.subject: processor, person acting under the authority of the controller or processor

obligations.description: Controllers and, where applicable, their representatives must maintain a record of processing activities under their responsibility, including specified details such as contact information, purposes, data categories, recipients, transfers, erasure time limits, and security measures.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Where applicable, the controller’s representative must maintain a record of processing activities under the controller’s responsibility.
obligations.subject: controller’s representative

obligations.description: Processors and, where applicable, their representatives must maintain a record of all categories of processing activities carried out on behalf of a controller.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: Where applicable, the processor’s representative must maintain a record of processing activities carried out on behalf of a controller.
obligations.subject: processor’s representative

obligations.description: The controller and processor (and their representatives, where applicable) must cooperate with the supervisory authority upon request in the performance of its tasks.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk of processing personal data.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor shall implement pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data as an appropriate security measure.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor shall ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems and services.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor shall have the ability to restore the availability and access to personal data in a timely manner following a physical or technical incident.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor shall establish a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of security measures.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: In assessing security levels, account shall be taken of risks from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, disclosure, or access to personal data.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: Adherence to an approved code of conduct or certification mechanism may be used to demonstrate compliance with security requirements.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: The controller must notify the supervisory authority of a personal data breach without undue delay and, where feasible, within 72 hours of becoming aware of it, unless the breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of individuals.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: If the controller fails to notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours, it must provide reasons for the delay.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The processor must notify the controller without undue delay after becoming aware of a personal data breach.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: The controller's notification to the supervisory authority must include a description of the nature of the breach, including the categories and approximate number of affected data subjects and records.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller's notification to the supervisory authority must include the name and contact details of the data protection officer or another contact point for further information.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller's notification to the supervisory authority must describe the likely consequences of the personal data breach.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller's notification to the supervisory authority must describe the measures taken or proposed to address and mitigate the breach.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: If all required information cannot be provided at the same time, the controller may provide the information in phases without undue further delay.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must communicate a personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay when the breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The communication to the data subject must describe the nature of the breach in clear and plain language and include at least the information and measures specified in Article 33(3)(b), (c), and (d).
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from communicating the breach to the data subject if appropriate technical and organizational protection measures (e.g., encryption) were applied to render the data unintelligible.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from communicating the breach to the data subject if subsequent measures have eliminated the high risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller is exempt from communicating the breach to the data subject if doing so would involve disproportionate effort, provided an equally effective alternative measure (e.g., public communication) is used instead.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must communicate the breach to the data subject if it has not already done so, even if previously exempt under paragraph 3.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must carry out a data protection impact assessment prior to processing that is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, particularly when using new technologies.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must seek the advice of the data protection officer when one has been designated, during the conduct of a data protection impact assessment.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A data protection impact assessment is particularly required for systematic and extensive automated processing involving profiling that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects on individuals.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A data protection impact assessment is particularly required for large-scale processing of special categories of personal data.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller must consult the supervisory authority before processing if a data protection impact assessment indicates a high risk that has not been adequately mitigated.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The supervisory authority must provide written advice to the controller (and processor, if applicable) within eight weeks (extendable by six weeks) if it believes the processing infringes the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The supervisory authority must inform the controller and processor of any extension to the consultation period within one month of receiving the request, along with reasons for the delay.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The supervisory authority may suspend the consultation period until it receives requested information from the controller.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The controller must provide the supervisory authority with details of the responsibilities of the controller, joint controllers, and processors involved in the processing when seeking consultation.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The controller and processor must designate a data protection officer if processing is carried out by a public authority or body (excluding courts acting judicially).
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor must designate a data protection officer if their core activities involve regular and systematic monitoring of data subjects on a large scale.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor must designate a data protection officer if their core activities involve large-scale processing of special categories of data under Article 9 or data relating to criminal convictions and offences under Article 10.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: A group of undertakings may appoint a single data protection officer provided that the officer is easily accessible from each establishment.
obligations.subject: group of undertakings

obligations.description: A single data protection officer may be designated for multiple public authorities or bodies, considering their organisational structure and size.
obligations.subject: public authority or body

obligations.description: Controllers or processors not covered by paragraph 1 may, or where required by Union or Member State law shall, designate a data protection officer.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: Associations and other bodies representing categories of controllers or processors may designate a data protection officer.
obligations.subject: associations and other bodies representing controllers or processors

obligations.description: The controller and processor must involve the data protection officer properly and in a timely manner in all issues relating to personal data protection.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor must provide the data protection officer with necessary resources, access to personal data and processing operations, and support to maintain expert knowledge.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor must not give instructions to the data protection officer regarding the exercise of their tasks.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The controller and processor must not dismiss or penalize the data protection officer for performing their tasks.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: The data protection officer must directly report to the highest management level of the controller or processor.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: Data subjects have the right to contact the data protection officer regarding issues related to the processing of their personal data and the exercise of their rights under the GDPR.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The data protection officer must be bound by secrecy or confidentiality regarding the performance of their tasks, as required by Union or Member State law.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: The controller or processor must ensure that any additional tasks assigned to the data protection officer do not result in a conflict of interests.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: Inform and advise the controller or processor and their employees about their obligations under GDPR and other data protection laws.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: Monitor compliance with GDPR, other Union or Member State data protection provisions, and the controller's or processor's data protection policies.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: Provide advice on data protection impact assessments and monitor their performance as required by Article 35.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: Cooperate with the supervisory authority in the performance of their tasks.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: Act as the contact point for the supervisory authority on processing-related issues, including prior consultations under Article 36.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: Take into account the risk associated with processing operations, considering the nature, scope, context, and purposes of processing when performing tasks.
obligations.subject: data protection officer

obligations.description: Member States, supervisory authorities, the Board, and the Commission shall encourage the development of codes of conduct to promote proper application of the GDPR, considering sector-specific features and needs of micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises.
obligations.subject: Member States, supervisory authorities, the Board, and the Commission

obligations.description: Associations and other bodies representing controllers or processors may prepare, amend, or extend codes of conduct to specify the application of the GDPR in areas such as fair processing, data collection, pseudonymisation, data subject rights, child protection, security measures, breach notificatio
obligations.subject: Associations and other bodies representing controllers or processors

obligations.description: Associations and other bodies preparing a code of conduct or amending/extending an existing one must submit the draft to the competent supervisory authority under Article 55.
obligations.subject: associations and other bodies

obligations.description: The supervisory authority must provide an opinion on whether the draft code, amendment, or extension complies with the GDPR and approve it if it provides sufficient appropriate safeguards.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Where the code does not relate to processing in multiple Member States, the supervisory authority must register and publish the approved code.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Where the draft code relates to processing in several Member States, the competent supervisory authority must submit it to the Board for an opinion via the Article 63 procedure before approval.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The Board must provide an opinion on whether the draft code, amendment, or extension complies with the GDPR or provides appropriate safeguards when submitted under Article 63.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: A body monitoring compliance with a code of conduct must have appropriate expertise in the subject-matter and be accredited by the competent supervisory authority.
obligations.subject: body monitoring compliance

obligations.description: A body accredited to monitor compliance must demonstrate its independence and expertise to the satisfaction of the competent supervisory authority.
obligations.subject: body monitoring compliance

obligations.description: A body accredited to monitor compliance must establish procedures to assess eligibility of controllers and processors, monitor their compliance, and periodically review the code's operation.
obligations.subject: body monitoring compliance

obligations.description: A body accredited to monitor compliance must establish transparent procedures and structures to handle complaints about code infringements or implementation.
obligations.subject: body monitoring compliance

obligations.description: A body accredited to monitor compliance must demonstrate to the supervisory authority that its tasks do not create a conflict of interest.
obligations.subject: body monitoring compliance

obligations.description: The competent supervisory authority must submit draft criteria for accrediting bodies that monitor compliance with codes of conduct.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Certification bodies must inform the supervisory authority before issuing or renewing certification to allow the authority to exercise its powers under Article 58(2)(h).
obligations.subject: certification bodies

obligations.description: Member States must ensure that certification bodies are accredited by either the competent supervisory authority or the national accreditation body complying with Regulation (EC) No 765/2008 and EN-ISO/IEC 17065/2012.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Certification bodies must demonstrate independence and expertise in data protection to the satisfaction of the competent supervisory authority to be accredited.
obligations.subject: certification bodies

obligations.description: Certification bodies must undertake to respect the certification criteria approved by the supervisory authority or the Board under Article 42(5) and Article 63.
obligations.subject: certification bodies

obligations.description: Certification bodies must establish procedures for issuing, periodically reviewing, and withdrawing data protection certification, seals, and marks.
obligations.subject: certification bodies

obligations.description: Transfers of personal data to a third country or international organisation must only occur if the conditions in Chapter V of the GDPR are complied with.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: Controllers and processors must ensure that conditions for onward transfers from a third country or international organisation to another third country or international organisation are met.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: All provisions in Chapter V must be applied to ensure the level of protection for natural persons under the GDPR is not undermined.
obligations.subject: controller and processor

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation may only occur if the European Commission has decided that the recipient ensures an adequate level of data protection.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Transfers to a third country or international organisation with an adequate level of protection do not require specific authorisation.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: The European Commission must assess the adequacy of data protection in a third country or international organisation by considering rule of law, human rights, legislation, data protection rules, security measures, case-law, and effective rights and redress for data subjects.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The European Commission must evaluate the existence and effective functioning of one or more independent supervisory authorities in the third country or international organisation responsible for enforcing data protection compliance.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: A controller or processor may transfer personal data to a third country or international organisation only if appropriate safeguards are provided and enforceable data subject rights and effective legal remedies are available.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Appropriate safeguards may be provided without supervisory authority authorisation through legally binding instruments between public authorities.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Appropriate safeguards may be provided without supervisory authority authorisation through binding corporate rules in accordance with Article 47.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Appropriate safeguards may be provided without supervisory authority authorisation through standard data protection clauses adopted by the Commission.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Appropriate safeguards may be provided without supervisory authority authorisation through standard data protection clauses adopted by a supervisory authority and approved by the Commission.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Appropriate safeguards may be provided without supervisory authority authorisation through an approved code of conduct together with binding commitments by the controller or processor in the third country.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Appropriate safeguards may be provided without supervisory authority authorisation through an approved certification mechanism together with binding commitments by the controller or processor in the third country.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Binding corporate rules must be legally binding and enforced by all members of the group of undertakings or enterprises, including employees.
obligations.subject: group of undertakings or enterprises

obligations.description: Binding corporate rules must expressly confer enforceable rights on data subjects regarding the processing of their personal data.
obligations.subject: group of undertakings or enterprises

obligations.description: Binding corporate rules must specify the structure and contact details of the group and its members.
obligations.subject: group of undertakings or enterprises

obligations.description: Binding corporate rules must detail the data transfers, including categories of personal data, processing purposes, types of data subjects, and third countries involved.
obligations.subject: group of undertakings or enterprises

obligations.description: Binding corporate rules must have a legally binding nature both internally and externally.
obligations.subject: group of undertakings or enterprises

obligations.description: Binding corporate rules must apply the general data protection principles, including purpose limitation, data minimisation, storage limits, data quality, privacy by design, legal basis for processing, special category data handling, security measures, and restrictions on onward transfers.
obligations.subject: group of undertakings or enterprises

obligations.description: A controller or processor may only recognise or enforce a third-country court or administrative decision requiring transfer or disclosure of personal data if it is based on an international agreement between the third country and the Union or a Member State.
obligations.subject: controller or processor

obligations.description: Third-country judicial or administrative decisions requiring personal data transfers are prohibited from being recognised or enforced unless based on an applicable international agreement.
obligations.subject: any entity recognizing or enforcing third-country decisions

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation may only occur if the data subject has given explicit consent after being informed of the risks due to the absence of an adequacy decision or appropriate safeguards.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation is permitted if it is necessary for the performance of a contract between the data subject and the controller or for pre-contractual measures at the data subject’s request.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation is permitted if it is necessary for the conclusion or performance of a contract concluded in the interest of the data subject between the controller and another party.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation is permitted if it is necessary for important reasons of public interest.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation is permitted if it is necessary for the establishment, exercise, or defence of legal claims.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation is permitted if it is necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or other persons, and the data subject is unable to give consent.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: A transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation is permitted if it is made from a register intended by Union or Member State law to provide public information and is open to consultation by the public or any person.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: The Commission and supervisory authorities shall develop international cooperation mechanisms to facilitate effective enforcement of personal data protection legislation.
obligations.subject: Commission and supervisory authorities

obligations.description: The Commission and supervisory authorities shall provide international mutual assistance in enforcing personal data protection legislation, including through notification, complaint referral, investigative assistance, and information exchange, with appropriate safeguards.
obligations.subject: Commission and supervisory authorities

obligations.description: The Commission and supervisory authorities shall engage relevant stakeholders in discussions and activities to further international cooperation in enforcing personal data protection legislation.
obligations.subject: Commission and supervisory authorities

obligations.description: The Commission and supervisory authorities shall promote the exchange and documentation of personal data protection legislation and practices, including on jurisdictional conflicts with third countries.
obligations.subject: Commission and supervisory authorities

obligations.description: Each Member State shall establish one or more independent public authorities to monitor GDPR application and protect fundamental rights related to data processing.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each supervisory authority shall cooperate with other supervisory authorities and the Commission to ensure consistent application of the GDPR throughout the Union.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Where multiple supervisory authorities exist in a Member State, the Member State shall designate one to represent them in the Board and establish a mechanism to ensure compliance with the consistency mechanism.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each Member State shall notify the Commission of its national laws adopted under this Chapter by 25 May 2018, and promptly notify any subsequent amendments.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each supervisory authority shall act with complete independence in performing its tasks and exercising its powers under the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Members of each supervisory authority shall remain free from external influence, direct or indirect, and shall not seek or take instructions from anyone.
obligations.subject: member or members of supervisory authority

obligations.description: Members of each supervisory authority shall refrain from actions incompatible with their duties and shall not engage in any incompatible occupation during their term of office.
obligations.subject: member or members of supervisory authority

obligations.description: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is provided with the necessary human, technical, and financial resources, premises, and infrastructure for effective performance of its tasks.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority selects its own staff, who are subject exclusively to the direction of the supervisory authority's members.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is subject to financial control that does not affect its independence and has separate, public annual budgets.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Member States must appoint each member of their supervisory authority through a transparent procedure by parliament, government, head of state, or an independent body designated by national law.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Each member of the supervisory authority must possess the qualifications, experience, and skills necessary, particularly in personal data protection, to perform their duties.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: The duties of a supervisory authority member end upon expiry of term, resignation, or compulsory retirement as defined by national law.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: A member of the supervisory authority may only be dismissed for serious misconduct or if they no longer meet the required conditions for performing their duties.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Each Member State shall establish a supervisory authority by law.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each Member State shall define the qualifications and eligibility conditions for appointment as a member of the supervisory authority.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each Member State shall establish rules and procedures for the appointment of supervisory authority members.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: The term of office for supervisory authority members shall be no less than four years, with exceptions for initial staggered appointments after 24 May 2016.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each Member State shall determine whether and for how many terms supervisory authority members may be reappointed.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Each Member State shall define the obligations, prohibitions on incompatible actions, occupations, and benefits, and rules for cessation of employment for supervisory authority members and staff.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: Supervisory authority members and staff shall be subject to a duty of professional secrecy regarding confidential information obtained during or after their term of office.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority members and staff

obligations.description: The supervisory authority of the main or single establishment of a controller or processor shall act as the lead supervisory authority for cross-border processing under Article 60.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Each supervisory authority may handle complaints or infringements that relate solely to an establishment in its Member State or substantially affect data subjects only in its Member State.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: A supervisory authority handling a case under paragraph 2 must inform the lead supervisory authority without delay.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The lead supervisory authority must decide within three weeks whether to handle a case informed by another authority, based on the presence of an establishment in the relevant Member State.
obligations.subject: lead supervisory authority

obligations.description: A supervisory authority that informed the lead supervisory authority may submit a draft decision to the lead authority.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Monitor and enforce the application of the GDPR within their territory.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Promote public awareness and understanding of risks, rules, safeguards, and rights related to data processing, with specific attention to children.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Advise national parliament, government, and other institutions on legislative and administrative measures concerning the protection of natural persons' rights and freedoms regarding processing.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Promote awareness among controllers and processors of their obligations under the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Provide information to any data subject concerning the exercise of their rights under the GDPR and cooperate with other supervisory authorities when appropriate.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Handle complaints lodged by data subjects or authorized bodies, investigate them appropriately, and inform the complainant of progress and outcomes within a reasonable time.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Cooperate with and provide mutual assistance to other supervisory authorities, including sharing information, to ensure consistent application of the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must order controllers and processors to provide any information required for performing their tasks.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must carry out data protection audits.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must review certifications issued under Article 42(7).
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must notify controllers or processors of alleged infringements of the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must obtain access to all personal data and information necessary for performing their tasks from controllers and processors.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must obtain access to premises, data processing equipment, and means of controllers and processors in accordance with Union or Member State procedural law.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must issue warnings to controllers or processors when intended processing operations are likely to infringe the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must issue reprimands to controllers or processors where processing operations have infringed the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must order controllers or processors to comply with data subjects' requests to exercise their rights under the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must order controllers or processors to bring processing operations into compliance with the GDPR where appropriate.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Each supervisory authority shall draw up an annual report on its activities, which may include a list of types of infringements notified and measures taken under Article 58(2).
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities shall transmit their annual reports to the national parliament, the government, and other authorities designated by Member State law.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities shall make their annual reports available to the public, the European Commission, and the Board.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The lead supervisory authority must cooperate with other supervisory authorities concerned to reach consensus and exchange all relevant information.
obligations.subject: lead supervisory authority

obligations.description: The lead supervisory authority may request mutual assistance from other supervisory authorities under Article 61 and conduct joint operations under Article 62.
obligations.subject: lead supervisory authority

obligations.description: The lead supervisory authority must promptly communicate relevant information to other supervisory authorities concerned and submit a draft decision for their opinion, taking due account of their views.
obligations.subject: lead supervisory authority

obligations.description: If a supervisory authority concerned raises a relevant and reasoned objection within four weeks and the lead supervisory authority does not accept it, the lead supervisory authority must refer the matter to the consistency mechanism under Article 63.
obligations.subject: lead supervisory authority

obligations.description: Lead and other concerned supervisory authorities must exchange information required under Article 66 using electronic means in a standardized format.
obligations.subject: lead supervisory authority and other supervisory authorities concerned

obligations.description: The Commission may specify the format, procedures, and arrangements for mutual assistance and electronic exchange of information between supervisory authorities and the Board via implementing acts.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities may immediately adopt provisional measures in exceptional circumstances to protect data subjects' rights and freedoms, derogating from standard consistency mechanisms.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority concerned

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must provide each other with relevant information and mutual assistance to ensure consistent application of the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must implement measures for effective cooperation with one another.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Each supervisory authority must respond to a request from another supervisory authority without undue delay and no later than one month.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Requests for mutual assistance must contain all necessary information, including the purpose and reasons for the request.
obligations.subject: requesting supervisory authority

obligations.description: Information exchanged under mutual assistance must be used only for the purpose for which it was requested.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: A supervisory authority may refuse a request for mutual assistance only if it is not competent or if compliance would infringe applicable law.
obligations.subject: requested supervisory authority

obligations.description: The requested supervisory authority must inform the requesting authority of the results of the assistance provided.
obligations.subject: requested supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities shall conduct joint operations, including joint investigations and enforcement measures, when appropriate and involving members or staff from other Member States.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities of Member States where the controller or processor has establishments or where a significant number of data subjects are likely to be substantially affected have the right to participate in joint operations.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The supervisory authority competent under Article 56(1) or (4) shall invite other relevant supervisory authorities to participate in joint operations and respond without delay to their requests to participate.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: A supervisory authority may, under Member State law and with authorization, confer investigative powers on members or staff of a seconding supervisory authority involved in joint operations.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Members or staff of a seconding supervisory authority may exercise their investigative powers in the host Member State, but only if permitted by the host Member State’s law and under the guidance and presence of the host authority’s members or staff.
obligations.subject: seconding supervisory authority's members or staff

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities shall cooperate with each other and, where relevant, with the Commission to ensure consistent application of the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authorities

obligations.description: The Board shall adopt a binding decision when a supervisory authority raises a relevant and reasoned objection to a draft decision of the lead authority or the lead authority rejects such an objection.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall adopt a binding decision when there are conflicting views on which supervisory authority is competent for the main establishment.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall adopt a binding decision when a competent supervisory authority fails to request its opinion under Article 64(1) or fails to follow its opinion.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Any supervisory authority concerned or the Commission may communicate matters to the Board when a supervisory authority does not request or follow its opinion under Article 64.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority concerned or the Commission

obligations.description: The Board shall adopt binding decisions within one month of referral, extendable by one additional month for complexity.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Binding decisions adopted by the Board shall be reasoned and addressed to the lead supervisory authority and all concerned supervisory authorities.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Binding decisions adopted by the Board shall be binding on the lead supervisory authority and all concerned supervisory authorities.
obligations.subject: lead supervisory authority and concerned supervisory authorities

obligations.description: The Commission may adopt implementing acts to specify standardized electronic exchange arrangements for information between supervisory authorities and the Board.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The European Data Protection Board is established as a body of the Union with legal personality.
obligations.subject: European Data Protection Board

obligations.description: The Board shall be represented by its Chair.
obligations.subject: European Data Protection Board

obligations.description: The Board shall be composed of the head of one supervisory authority of each Member State and the European Data Protection Supervisor, or their representatives.
obligations.subject: Member States and European Data Protection Supervisor

obligations.description: Where a Member State has multiple supervisory authorities, it shall appoint a joint representative in accordance with its national law.
obligations.subject: Member State

obligations.description: The Commission shall have the right to participate in the Board’s activities and meetings without voting rights and shall designate a representative.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The Chair of the Board shall communicate the Board’s activities to the Commission.
obligations.subject: Chair of the European Data Protection Board

obligations.description: The European Data Protection Supervisor shall have voting rights only on decisions concerning principles and rules applicable to Union institutions, bodies, offices, and agencies that correspond to this Regulation.
obligations.subject: European Data Protection Supervisor

obligations.description: The Board must act independently when performing its tasks or exercising its powers under Articles 70 and 71.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall not seek or take instructions from anybody when performing its tasks or exercising its powers, except for specific Commission requests.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Ensure the consistent application of the GDPR.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Monitor and ensure the correct application of the GDPR in cases under Articles 64 and 65, without prejudice to national supervisory authorities.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Advise the Commission on any issue related to personal data protection in the Union, including proposed amendments to the GDPR.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Advise the Commission on the format and procedures for exchanging information regarding binding corporate rules.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Issue guidelines, recommendations, and best practices on procedures for erasing links, copies, or replications of personal data from publicly available communication services.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Examine, on its own initiative or upon request, any question regarding the application of the GDPR and issue guidelines, recommendations, and best practices to encourage consistent application.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Issue guidelines, recommendations, and best practices for specifying criteria and conditions for decisions based on profiling under Article 22(2).
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall consult interested parties and provide them with a reasonable opportunity to comment.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall make the results of the consultation procedure publicly available.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall draw up an annual report on the protection of natural persons regarding processing in the Union and, where relevant, in third countries and international organisations.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall make the annual report public.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall transmit the annual report to the European Parliament, the Council, and the Commission.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The annual report shall include a review of the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations, and best practices referred to in point (l) of Article 70.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall take decisions by a simple majority of its members, unless otherwise provided for in this Regulation.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall adopt its own rules of procedure by a two-thirds majority of its members.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall organise its own operational arrangements.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall elect a chair and two deputy chairs from among its members by simple majority.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The term of office of the Chair and deputy chairs shall be five years and renewable once.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Chair shall convene meetings of the Board and prepare its agenda.
obligations.subject: Chair

obligations.description: The Chair shall notify decisions adopted by the Board pursuant to Article 65 to the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned.
obligations.subject: Chair

obligations.description: The Chair shall ensure the timely performance of the Board's tasks, particularly in relation to the consistency mechanism under Article 63.
obligations.subject: Chair

obligations.description: The Board shall lay down the allocation of tasks between the Chair and the deputy chairs in its rules of procedure.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: The Board shall ensure confidentiality of its discussions when deemed necessary according to its rules of procedure.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Access to documents submitted to Board members, experts, and third-party representatives shall be governed by Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001.
obligations.subject: Board

obligations.description: Every data subject has the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority if they believe their personal data processing violates the GDPR.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: The supervisory authority must inform the complainant about the progress and outcome of the complaint, including the possibility of a judicial remedy.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Each natural or legal person has the right to an effective judicial remedy against a legally binding decision of a supervisory authority concerning them.
obligations.subject: natural or legal person

obligations.description: Each data subject has the right to an effective judicial remedy if the competent supervisory authority does not handle a complaint or does not inform them within three months of its progress or outcome.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Proceedings against a supervisory authority must be brought before the courts of the Member State where the supervisory authority is established.
obligations.subject: natural or legal person or data subject

obligations.description: The supervisory authority must forward any prior opinion or decision of the Board to the court when proceedings are brought against its decision that followed the consistency mechanism.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Each data subject has the right to an effective judicial remedy if they consider their GDPR rights have been infringed by non-compliant processing of their personal data.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Proceedings against a controller or processor must be brought in the courts of the Member State where the controller or processor has an establishment.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Data subjects may alternatively bring proceedings in the courts of the Member State where they have their habitual residence, unless the controller or processor is a public authority acting in the exercise of its public powers.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Data subjects have the right to mandate a not-for-profit body, organisation, or association with public interest statutory objectives and activity in data protection to lodge complaints and exercise rights on their behalf.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Not-for-profit bodies, organisations, or associations meeting specified criteria may act on behalf of data subjects to lodge complaints and exercise rights under Articles 77, 78, 79, and 82.
obligations.subject: not-for-profit body, organisation or association

obligations.description: Member States may permit not-for-profit bodies, organisations, or associations to lodge complaints and exercise rights under Articles 77, 78, and 79 without a specific data subject mandate if they consider data subject rights have been infringed.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: A competent court that has information about pending proceedings on the same subject matter involving the same controller or processor in another Member State must contact that court to confirm the existence of the proceedings.
obligations.subject: competent court

obligations.description: A competent court other than the court first seized may suspend its proceedings when the same subject matter involving the same controller or processor is pending in another Member State's court.
obligations.subject: competent court other than the court first seized

obligations.description: A court other than the court first seized may decline jurisdiction upon application by a party, if the court first seized has jurisdiction and its law permits consolidation of the proceedings.
obligations.subject: competent court other than the court first seized

obligations.description: Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage due to a GDPR infringement has the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor.
obligations.subject: data subject

obligations.description: Controllers are liable for damage caused by processing that infringes the GDPR.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processors are liable for damage caused by processing only if they failed to comply with processor-specific GDPR obligations or acted outside or contrary to the controller's lawful instructions.
obligations.subject: processor

obligations.description: Controllers and processors are exempt from liability if they prove they are not in any way responsible for the damage-causing event.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: When multiple controllers or processors are involved in the same processing and are responsible for damage, each is liable for the entire damage to ensure effective compensation.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: A controller or processor that has paid full compensation is entitled to claim back from other involved parties their respective share of responsibility.
obligations.subject: controller, processor

obligations.description: Each supervisory authority must ensure that administrative fines for GDPR infringements are effective, proportionate, and dissuasive.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Administrative fines may be imposed in addition to or instead of other corrective measures listed in Article 58(2).
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: When deciding whether to impose a fine and its amount, supervisory authorities must consider the nature, gravity, and duration of the infringement, including the processing scope and impact on data subjects.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must consider whether the infringement was intentional or negligent when determining fines.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must take into account any actions taken by the controller or processor to mitigate damage to data subjects.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must assess the degree of responsibility of the controller or processor based on implemented technical and organizational measures under Articles 25 and 32.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must consider any previous infringements by the controller or processor when determining fines.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must consider the degree of cooperation by the controller or processor in remedying the infringement and mitigating adverse effects.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Supervisory authorities must take into account the categories of personal data affected by the infringement when determining fines.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: Member States must establish rules on penalties for infringements of the GDPR that are not subject to administrative fines under Article 83.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Member States must ensure that penalties for GDPR infringements are effective, proportionate, and dissuasive.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Member States must notify the European Commission of their national provisions adopted under paragraph 1 by 25 May 2018.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Member States must promptly notify the European Commission of any subsequent amendments to their national provisions adopted under paragraph 1.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Member States must enact laws to reconcile data protection rights with freedom of expression and information, including for journalistic, academic, artistic, or literary purposes.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Member States must provide exemptions or derogations from key GDPR chapters for processing related to journalistic, academic, artistic, or literary expression when necessary to balance data protection and freedom of expression.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Member States must notify the European Commission of their national laws adopted under paragraph 2, and any subsequent amendments to those laws.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Public authorities or bodies may disclose personal data in official documents in accordance with Union or Member State law to reconcile public access with data protection rights.
obligations.subject: public authority or public body or private body performing a public interest task

obligations.description: Member States may establish specific conditions for the processing of national identification numbers or other general identifiers.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: National identification numbers or other general identifiers must only be processed under appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of the data subject.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Member States may enact specific rules via law or collective agreements to ensure protection of employees' rights and freedoms regarding personal data processing in employment contexts.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Member States must include suitable and specific measures in their rules to safeguard employees' human dignity, legitimate interests, and fundamental rights, particularly concerning transparency, intra-group data transfers, and workplace monitoring.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Each Member State must notify the European Commission of its national provisions adopted under paragraph 1 by 25 May 2018, and promptly notify any subsequent amendments.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Processing for archiving, scientific, historical research, or statistical purposes must implement appropriate technical and organizational safeguards to ensure respect for data minimisation and protect data subjects' rights and freedoms.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Pseudonymisation may be used as a safeguard where it allows the archiving, scientific, historical research, or statistical purposes to be fulfilled.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Processing for archiving, scientific, historical research, or statistical purposes must be carried out in a way that avoids or ceases identification of data subjects if possible.
obligations.subject: controller

obligations.description: Union or Member State law may derogate from data subjects' rights under Articles 15, 16, 18, and 21 for scientific, historical research, or statistical purposes, provided such derogations are necessary and subject to safeguards.
obligations.subject: Union or Member State legislature

obligations.description: Member States may adopt specific rules to define the powers of supervisory authorities regarding controllers or processors bound by professional secrecy, where necessary and proportionate to balance data protection and secrecy obligations.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Rules adopted under paragraph 1 apply only to personal data received by controllers or processors in the course of activities covered by their obligation of secrecy.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Each Member State must notify the Commission of rules adopted under paragraph 1 by 25 May 2018, and any subsequent amendments without delay.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: Churches and religious associations may continue applying their existing comprehensive data protection rules if they are brought into line with the GDPR.
obligations.subject: churches and religious associations

obligations.description: Churches and religious associations applying comprehensive rules under Article 91(1) must be subject to supervision by an independent supervisory authority.
obligations.subject: churches and religious associations

obligations.description: The supervisory authority overseeing churches and religious associations must be independent and fulfill the conditions in Chapter VI of the GDPR.
obligations.subject: supervisory authority

obligations.description: The Commission may adopt delegated acts only under the conditions specified in this Article.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The delegation of power to the Commission is indeterminate from 24 May 2016 unless revoked.
obligations.subject: European Parliament and Council

obligations.description: The European Parliament or the Council may revoke the Commission's delegation of power at any time.
obligations.subject: European Parliament and Council

obligations.description: The Commission must notify the European Parliament and the Council simultaneously upon adopting a delegated act.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: A delegated act enters into force only if neither the European Parliament nor the Council objects within three months (or extended period).
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The European Parliament or the Council may extend the review period for a delegated act by three months.
obligations.subject: European Parliament and Council

obligations.description: This Regulation shall not impose additional obligations on natural or legal persons regarding processing connected to publicly available electronic communications services in public communication networks, where they are already subject to specific obligations under Directive 2002/58/EC with the sam
obligations.subject: natural or legal persons

obligations.description: International agreements on personal data transfers to third countries or international organisations concluded by Member States before 24 May 2016 and compliant with pre-2016 Union law shall remain in force.
obligations.subject: Member States

obligations.description: The Commission shall submit a report on the evaluation and review of the GDPR to the European Parliament and the Council by 25 May 2020 and every four years thereafter, and make the reports public.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The Commission shall examine the application and functioning of Chapter V on data transfers to third countries and international organisations, including decisions under Article 45(3) and Article 25(6) of Directive 95/46/EC.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The Commission shall examine the application and functioning of Chapter VII on cooperation and consistency.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The Commission may request information from Member States and supervisory authorities for the purpose of preparing the evaluation and review reports.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The Commission shall take into account the positions and findings of the European Parliament, the Council, and other relevant bodies or sources when conducting evaluations and reviews.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The Commission shall submit appropriate proposals to amend the GDPR if necessary, particularly in light of developments in information technology and the state of progress in the information society.
obligations.subject: Commission

obligations.description: The Commission shall submit legislative proposals to amend other Union legal acts on data protection to ensure uniform and consistent protection of natural persons regarding processing.
obligations.subject: Commission
